tossups.question: A member of this family had the exterior of a building and the loggia across the street decorated with his trademark symbol of a sail blowing in the wind. 30 marble squares are inlaid in a funerary chapel named for this family in the San Pancrazio. An artwork that is [emphasize] actually named for this family is “carried in procession” in a painting by Frederic Leighton, but is incorrectly attributed in the painting’s title. A patron from this family commissioned the white marble facade of the Santa Maria Novella from Leon Battista Alberti, who designed a (*) palazzo named for this family of wool merchants. A panel painting named for this family uses gold leaf and egg tempera to depict three angels on either side of the Virgin and Child and was made in 1285 in Siena. For 10 points, what Florentine family names a Madonna by Duccio?
tossups.question_sanitized: A member of this family had the exterior of a building and the loggia across the street decorated with his trademark symbol of a sail blowing in the wind. 30 marble squares are inlaid in a funerary chapel named for this family in the San Pancrazio. An artwork that is [emphasize] actually named for this family is "carried in procession" in a painting by Frederic Leighton, but is incorrectly attributed in the painting's title. A patron from this family commissioned the white marble facade of the Santa Maria Novella from Leon Battista Alberti, who designed a (*) palazzo named for this family of wool merchants. A panel painting named for this family uses gold leaf and egg tempera to depict three angels on either side of the Virgin and Child and was made in 1285 in Siena. For 10 points, what Florentine family names a Madonna by Duccio?
tossups.answer: Rucellai (“ROO-chell-eye”) family [accept Rucellai Madonna; accept Palazzo Rucellai or Rucellai Chapel] (The Frederic Leighton painting is Cimabue’s Madonna Carried in Procession through the Streets of Florence.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Rucellai ("ROO-chell-eye") family [accept Rucellai Madonna; accept Palazzo Rucellai or Rucellai Chapel] (The Frederic Leighton painting is Cimabue's Madonna Carried in Procession through the Streets of Florence.)

bonuses.leadin: This painting is displayed in the Uffizi beside Giotto’s Ognissanti Madonna and is partially named for a family of wool merchants. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This painting is displayed in the Uffizi beside Giotto's Ognissanti Madonna and is partially named for a family of wool merchants. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this panel painting from Siena that depicts three angels on each side of a Virgin Mary and child.
The Rucellai (“ROO-chell-eye”) Madonna is by this Sienese painter, who painted forty-three scenes from the life of Jesus around the enthroned Virgin Mary in his Maestà.
Both Duccio’s Rucellai Madonna and his Maestà were commissioned as this kind of artwork. These religious works include one named for Isenheim by Matthias Grünewald.
parts_sanitized: Name this panel painting from Siena that depicts three angels on each side of a Virgin Mary and child.
The Rucellai ("ROO-chell-eye") Madonna is by this Sienese painter, who painted forty-three scenes from the life of Jesus around the enthroned Virgin Mary in his Maesta.
Both Duccio's Rucellai Madonna and his Maesta were commissioned as this kind of artwork. These religious works include one named for Isenheim by Matthias Grunewald.

packet.name: D - UBC B, Oxford A, Waterloo B, CWRU A, Columbia C
